data.title: ST Engineering's 2029 Targets Imply Steady Long-Term Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore Technologies Engineering's 2029 targets imply steady long-term growth, RHB Research's Shekhar Jaiswal says in a research report. The technology, defense and engineering group's successful achievements of its previous five-year targets suggest it can attain its 8.6% revenue and up to 13.6% profit CAGR targets for 2024-2029, the analyst says. Its Commercial Aerospace segment is expected to reach S$6.0 billion revenue by 2029, thanks to factors including investments in next-generation capabilities in composite aerostructures, the analyst says. Its Defence & Public Security segment could deliver over S$7.5 billion revenue by 2029, partly due to expanding its international defence business. RHB raises the stock's target price to S$7.80 from S$5.90 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 5.2% higher at S$6.91. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: JOYY Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• JOYY Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.77​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.01. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.18 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 98 cents to $1.30 per share.

  • Revenue fell 3.6% to $549.45 million from a year ago; analysts expected $549.46 million.

  • JOYY Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $5.67​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $304.49 million.

  • JOYY Inc shares had risen by 14.0% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 2.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 9 "strong buy" or "buy," 5 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the online services peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for JOYY Inc is $57.10

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data March 20 at 03:21 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: New Talisman Gold Delays Production in Talisman Mine to April; Shares Hit Four-Year High
data.description:
New Talisman Gold Mines will now enter production at its Talisman mine in April instead of the first calendar quarter, according to a Thursday New Zealand bourse filing.

The delay will allow the company to feed more "meaningful volumes" into its newly commissioned ore processing plant and optimize operations, the filing said.

There have been no major issues with assembling the plant, but fine-tuning is still needed for optimal ore recovery, the filing added.

The company said it plans to scale up processing volumes in May to about 300 tons per month.

The company's shares rose almost 15% in recent Thursday trade, hitting their highest since January 2021.

data.title: Mapletree Logistics Trust Completes Divestment of Malaysian Property
data.description:
Mapletree Logistics Trust completed the divestment of a property located in Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia named Linfox, for 71 million ringgit, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The property comprises a single story warehouse and a double story office and has a net lettable area of 17,984 square meters.

The divestment is part of the trust's strategy to rejuvenate its portfolio.

Following the divestment, the total portfolio stands at 180 properties while assets under manager is SG$13.3 billion.

Shares of the trust were up nearly 2% in recent trading.

data.title: IOI's 3Q Earnings Could Improve on Quarter — Market Talk
data.description: IOI's fiscal 3Q net profit could improve sequentially as fair-value losses on derivatives reverse, AmInvestment Bank's Gan Huey Ling says in a note. Manufacturing EBIT is set to rise, with higher selling prices for oleochemical products offsetting rising palm kernel oil costs, the analyst says. However, refining margins could remain under pressure due to Indonesian competition, she says. Plantation earnings are likely to fall on quarter due to a projected 20%-25% drop in fresh-fruit-bunch production, she adds. Gan expects IOI's FY 2025 crude palm oil production costs to be flat at 2,500 ringgit a ton, with lower fertilizer costs offset by higher wages. AmInvestment Bank maintains a buy rating on IOI with a target price of MYR5.00. Shares are 0.5% lower at MYR3.72. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Weakens After GDP Data
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar fell to around $0.579 on Thursday, even after data showed the economy has emerged from recession.

New Zealand's economy grew by 0.7% quarter-on-quarter in Q4, surpassing analysts' expectations of a 0.4% rise and the Reserve Bank’s forecast of 0.3%, following a revised 1.1% contraction in Q3.

Meanwhile, annual GDP fell 1.1%, slightly better than the expected 1.4% decline.

Despite the improvement, the RBNZ is unlikely to alter its planned rate cuts, as economic weakness persists and external headwinds, particularly the escalating trade tensions, continue to pose risks.

In February, the central bank lowered its official cash rate by 50bps to 3.75% and signaled an additional two 25-basis-point cuts in April and May.

Meanwhile, in the U.S., the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady on Wednesday and kept its forecast for two cuts amid high uncertainty about President Donald Trump’s tariffs.

data.title: Financial Times - March 20
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Financial Times. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

Headlines

- Brussels takes action against Google and Apple despite Trump threat

- EY cuts jobs in pullback from UK legal sector

- SoftBank expands AI portfolio with $6.5bn Ampere deal

- Deutsche Bank to axe 2,000 more jobs

Overview

- The European Commission is going ahead with regulatory action against Apple and Google under landmark legislation designed to expose the groups to new competition.

- EY is restructuring its UK legal arm, sparking a fresh round of job cuts and hastening the Big Four firm's retreat from its previous ambition to take a bigger slice of the lucrative sector.

- SoftBank has agreed to acquire chip start-up Ampere Computing for $6.5 billion as the Japanese tech group's founder Masayoshi Son expands his ambitions in artificial intelligence.

- Deutsche Bank's chief executive said the bank would axe almost 2,000 jobs in its retail division this year.

data.title: Kraken nears $1.5B deal allowing it to offer US crypto futures: Report
data.description:
Crypto exchange Kraken is reportedly closing in on a $1.5 billion acquisition of trading platform NinjaTrader, a move that would expand Kraken’s customer base and enable it to offer crypto futures and derivatives in the US.

The deal could be confirmed by the morning of March 20 in the US, The Wall Street Journal said in a March 19 report, citing people familiar with the matter.

Kraken’s expanded offerings would be made possible through NinjaTrader’s registration as a Futures Commission Merchant. 

The move would help Kraken’s strategy to work across several asset classes — including plans for equities trading and payments — while enabling NinjaTrader to expand into the UK, continental Europe and Australian markets, the sources told WSJ.

NinjaTrader is expected to remain a standalone platform under Kraken.

Cointelegraph reached out to Kraken and NinjaTrader for comment but did not receive an immediate response.

Kraken posted $1.5 billion in revenue and $665 billion in trading volume from 2.5 million funded customer accounts on its platform in 2024, while NinjaTrader recently said its futures trading tools are used by over 1.8 million customers.

Kraken announced its intention to broaden its product offerings and services last November when it shuttered its non-fungible token marketplace.

It comes as the US Securities and Exchange Commission dropped its lawsuit against Kraken on March 3 after it initially alleged that the crypto platform acted as an unregistered broker, dealer, exchange and clearing agency. 

The suit was dismissed with prejudice, with no admission of wrongdoing, no penalties paid and no changes to Kraken’s business. 

Kraken is one of many firms that stand to benefit from a more relaxed regulatory environment in the US under President Donald Trump, who has promised to make America the “crypto capital” of the world.

The crypto exchange was founded in 2011 by Thanh Luu, Michael Gronager and former CEO Jesse Powell, who handed the reins over to former data analytics executive Amir Orad last July.

Kraken consistently ranks among the top seven to 15 largest crypto exchanges by spot trading volume, handling between $390 million and $4.4 billion in daily trades over the past three months, according to CoinGecko data.

data.title: Xingyu Automotive Lighting Systems 2024 Profit Rises 28%; Shares Jump 10%
data.description:
Changzhou Xingyu Automotive Lighting Systems posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 1.41 billion yuan, or 4.9299 yuan per share, up 28% from 1.10 billion yuan, or 3.8579 yuan per share, the previous year.

The automotive lamp manufacturer's revenue rose 29% year over year to 13.3 billion yuan from 10.2 billion yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The company declared a cash dividend of 1.30 yuan per share for 2024.

Shares of Changzhou Xingyu Automotive Lighting Systems jumped 10% in recent trade.

data.title: LIC Housing Finance to issue 10-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's LIC Housing Finance plans to raise 70 billion rupees ($810 million), which includes 45 billion rupees of a greenshoe option through sale of bonds maturing in 10 years, three bankers said on Thursday.

The housing finance company has invited bids from bankers and investors for the issue on Friday, they said.

LIC Housing Finance did not reply to a Reuters email for comment.

data.title: Next-Generation Virtual Pet Toys Ferro Pets Head To Kickstarter
data.description:
Adam Blake launches an interactive toy pet that grows and shrinks.

Photo Courtesy of Proton Toys LLC

PHILADELPHIA, March 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Adam Blake today announced the launch of his Kickstarter campaign for Ferro Pets, an innovative line of interactive toy pets that physically grow when fed and shrink as they shed. This innovative toy redefines the virtual pet experience by introducing tangible, science-based interactions.

Adam Blake, founder of Proton Toys LLC, stated, "Ferro is unlike any other toy pet; it actually looks and feels like it’s really alive. We've taken the simple concept of a magnet attracting iron filings and turned it into a pet toy that can grow, shrink and interact with you. I hope to restore the feeling of awe and wonder from toys that seems to have been missing in the toy industry lately."

Blake has a significant social media following, with his most recent video showcasing the toy amassing over 16 million views between Youtube, Instagram and Tiktok. "It has been amazing to see the huge wave of support I’ve received in creating Ferro Pets," Blake added. "Tens of thousands of people from all over the world are signing up to support the Kickstarter launch."

The Ferro Pets product line will debut with Ferro, with 4 other pets to launch soon after. Each toy in the line will feature a unique principle of physics as the essence of what makes the toy unique. These characters have been carefully designed to be part of a comprehensive story that involves these pets being brought to Earth from Mars and other planets to be cared for by kids. Blake also plans to build a companion app for future versions of the toy that can keep track of the pets’ well-being and play interactive games with the pets.

The Kickstarter campaign, scheduled to launch on March 31, 2025, aims to raise funds for the final production and distribution of Ferro Pets. The campaign will offer early bird specials and exclusive backer perks, with the product expected to hit Amazon and other major retailers by September 2025.

Blake shares, "The overwhelming response to my social media videos, with over 16 million views across platforms, indicates a strong market desire for Ferro Pets. I’m so excited to launch Ferro Pets and so grateful for those that plan to back the Kickstarter so we can bring this toy to life."

For more information about Ferro Pets and to stay updated on the Kickstarter launch, visit www.ferropets.com.

About Proton Toys LLC

Proton Toys LLC is an Arizona-based toy company committed to developing advanced educational toys that spark curiosity and foster STEM learning. Founded by Adam Blake, the company strives to transform the toy industry by offering products that blend fascinating scientific principles with captivating play experiences.

Contact Information:

Adam Blake

Proton Toys LLC

www.ferropets.com

adam@ferropets.com

Arizona, USA

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/01756044-3c57-4ef2-8a29-634c4a73c3de

Next-Generation Virtual Pet Toys Ferro Pets Head To Kickstarter

Photo Courtesy of Proton Toys LLC

data.title: Shipbuilder raid gingerly tests geopolitics shift
data.description:
South Korea's Hanwha has snapped up 9.9% of Australia's $1 bln Austal and may double it, months after trying to buy the entire firm. Donald Trump's protectionist shift could make taking over the US navy supplier even harder. Less risky small bites are sensible for the moment.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @AntonyMCurrie on X

CONTEXT NEWS

South Korean conglomerate Hanwha revealed on March 18 that it had bought a 9.9% stake in Australian shipbuilder Austal for A$183 million ($116 million). The buyer also used derivatives to take an economic interest in an additional 9.9% of Austal.

Austal only confirmed Hanwha’s interest in April 2024, saying it had received a non-binding takeover offer valuing the equity at A$1 billion.

In the term sheet for the stake purchase, reported by the Australian Financial Review, Hanwha said that it has "no intentions of submitting a control proposal, or making a takeover bid for the company, at this time."

data.title: Foreign selling in Indian stocks continues unabated in first half of March
data.description:
Foreign selling of Indian stocks continued in the first half of March, with information technology and consumer goods accounting for a significant share, amid concerns over the health of the U.S. and Indian economies.

Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) net sold Indian stocks worth $3.5 billion during the period. IT stocks accounted for 69.34 billion rupees ($803 million) of the selling, while consumer stocks worth $591 million were offloaded, exchange data showed on Thursday.

FPIs have sold Indian shares worth $28 billion between October and March, which has triggered a 13% fall in Nifty 50 from record high levels hit on September 27, 2024.

The IT index fell 3.2% during the first half of the month, compared to the benchmark Nifty 50's 1.2% rise. The IT index confirmed a bear market on March 12, which is defined as a decline of 20% from the recent peak.

Worries of a potential slowdown in the U.S. economy and inflationary concerns amid President Donald Trump's erratic tariff campaign have dimmed the prospects of a near-term recovery in earnings in the sector, said two analysts.

India's IT sector derives a large chunk of revenue from clients in the United States.

Earlier in the month, Jefferies double-downgraded India's IT sector to "underweight" from "overweight," citing high valuations and U.S. economic risks, while Citi said it is too early to be constructive on IT as recent U.S. data suggested a tough near-term outlook.

Meanwhile, recent domestic policy measures such as the government's cut to income tax and the Reserve Bank of India's interest rate cut and actions to boost liquidity failed to ignite investors' interest. The FMCG index rose 2.3% in the first half of March, after dropping 23% in the previous five months.

"Benefits of subsidies to low-income households and the tax-cuts for mid-income households are well understood, the sector lacks incremental triggers and is expensive on relative valuations compared to other sectors," said analysts led by Amish Shah of BofA India.

Among sectors, financials, oil and gas stocks have seen the highest foreign outflows since October, followed by consumer and auto stocks.

($1 = 86.3520 Indian rupees)

data.title: Nvidia to invest billions in US chip production over four years, FT reports
data.description: Nvidia plans to invest hundreds of billions of dollars in U.S.-made chips and electronics over the next four years, CEO Jensen Huang told the Financial Times on Wednesday.

data.title: LS Electric's US Unit Signs KRW162.5 Billion Data Center Contract
data.description:
LS Electric's US subsidiary LS Electric America signed a data center contract with Big Tech Data Center PJT, the electric power equipment manufacturer said earlier this week.

The contract is valued at 162.5 billion won, the release said.

Shares of LS Electric fell nearly 2% in recent trade on Thursday.

data.title: Inca Minerals Raises Voting Power in Stunalara Metals to 73.5%
data.description:
Inca Minerals raised its voting power in its takeover target, Stunalara Metals, to 73.5%, equivalent to acceptances totaling nearly 33.5 million undiluted shares, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Further, Andrew Haythorpe, who holds roughly 18.7% of Stunalara shares on an undiluted basis and 18.3% on a fully diluted basis, has confirmed his intention to accept Inca Minerals' takeover offer, per the filing.

Haythorpe's acceptance is subject to there being no superior proposal and the independent expert concluding that the offer is either fair and reasonable, or not fair but reasonable, the filing stated.

Inca Minerals' offer is subject to the bidder acquiring a relevant interest in at least 90% of all Stunalara shares on issue on a fully diluted basis by April 4, unless extended.

data.title: Goodwill Entertainment's Subsidiary Completes Share Subscription
data.description:
Goodwill Entertainment's wholly owned subsidiary, Yakitori One, completed the subscription of 480,000 new redeemable non-convertible preference shares and 20,000 shares, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Following the completion of the subscription, the subsidiary's issued and paid up capital is now SG$800,000.

data.title: Flynn Gold Resumes Drilling at Tasmania Project; Shares Hit 52-Week Low
data.description:
Flynn Gold resumed drilling at its Golden Ridge project in Tasmania, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The 2,000-meter diamond drilling program will test in-fill and extensional targets, with drilling at the Trafalgar prospect aiming to upgrade infilled areas of the exploration target to an inferred resource, the filing said.

Shares fell 9% in afternoon trade Thursday, earlier hitting a 52-week low.

data.title: Sinoma Science & Technology's 2024 Profit Declines 60%
data.description:
Sinoma Science & Technology's attributable profit slid 60% to 891.9 million yuan in 2024 from 2.22 billion yuan, after adjustments, in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

Earnings per share at the advanced materials manufacturer decreased 60% to 0.5315 yuan from 1.3249 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income declined 7.4% to 24 billion yuan from 25.9 billion yuan a year earlier.

Shares slipped 1% during Thursday's morning trading.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Dow Climbs After FOMC Interest-Rate Decision

Stocks rose after the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, while lowering its forecasts for growth this year.

Fed Dims Economic Outlook, Citing Uncertainty Over Tariffs

Officials extend pause on interest rates while projecting higher inflation and unemployment.

U.K. Is Expected to Follow Fed With Rate Pause. Why the BOE Really Wants to Cut.

The British economy has been much weaker than that of the U.S., and inflation is still well above the BOE target.

How Trump's talk with Putin about Ukraine involves this top U.S. industry

Trump wants Europe to buy more of America's liquified natural gas. But tariffs get in the way.

Australian Job Market Strength Showing Cracks

Australia's economy shed jobs in February in what could be the first clear indication in some time that interest rates have remained too high for too long.

New Zealand's Economy Returns to Growth; Outlook Uncertain

New Zealand's economy exited an extended period of recession in the final months of last year supported by hefty cuts in official interest rates, but the path ahead is uncertain for the small commodity-exporting nation given recent downgrades to world growth and a widening global trade war.

Canadian Consumers, Retailers Change Tack to Counter Tariff Stresses

Canadian consumers are responding to U.S. tariffs and presidential musings of annexation with a "Buy Canadian" movement that retailers and producers are swiftly embracing.

The Fed's interest-rate pause has a silver lining for worried Americans - if they make this move

Consumers are increasingly concerned about their jobs and their access to credit as recession fears loom.

Bonds Are Holding Up, but the Playbook Is Changing

As stocks slid in recent weeks, bond prices have ticked up, offsetting some losses

EU Targets 15% Cut in Steel Imports Amid U.S. Tariffs, Global Oversupply

The European Commission will protect the bloc's steel industry and introduce long-term measures to preserve its competitiveness in an industry awash in overcapacity globally.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Building-Products Distributor QXO Clinches Deal for Beacon Roofing

The agreement comes after a hostile takeover attempt.

SoftBank to Buy Ampere Computing for $6.5 Billion

The Japanese investment holding company said that the purchase would help to enhance its capabilities in key artificial-intelligence areas and accelerate its growth initiatives.

Bondi's Campaign Against Domestic Terrorism Starts With Tesla Foes

The attorney general is declaring a Justice Department crackdown on what she views as politically motivated attacks against Tesla CEO Elon Musk.

Chevron CEO Lobbies for More Time to Wind Down Venezuela Operations

Chief Executive Mike Wirth has lobbied top administration officials including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

Crypto Exchange Kraken Nears $1.5 Billion Deal for Futures Trading Business

NinjaTrader, a futures trading platform, would expand Kraken's offerings.

Ford Looks to Innovate Faster With AI Agents and Nvidia GPUs

Bryan Goodman, Ford Motor's director of artificial intelligence, said he's pushing forward a number of use cases for artificial intelligence at the company, as part of an effort to design and engineer new cars as quickly as its competitors.

Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner Resigns

The passenger railroad's chief executive is leaving the post after a little more than three years in the role.

Nissan to Cut 20% of Top Management Positions

Nissan said its top management positions will transition to a single-layer, non-officer framework, under which all corporate officers will assume the title of corporate executive.

Ben & Jerry's Says CEO Ousted by Unilever Over Brand's Social Activism

In a court filing, the ice cream maker said Unilever removed David Stever as CEO without approval from its independent board.

EU Pushes Ahead With Big Tech Antitrust Enforcement

The European Commission moved forward with enforcement of its digital antitrust rules on Apple and Alphabet's Google amid rising trade tensions with the U.S. over treatment of American tech giants.

data.title: U.S. Tariffs Likely No Longer Dollar Bullish, Deutsche Bank Says — Market Talk
data.description: There are good reasons to argue that U.S. tariffs are no longer as dollar bullish as assumed at the start of the year, says George Saravelos, Global Head of FX Research at Deutsche Bank. With the historic German fiscal shift voted through it is very likely that the new German government front-loads fiscal easing to offset the impact of the U.S. tariffs, he says. Hence, the knock-on impact on growth is likely to be materially more muted. This conclusion, he says is valid across all of Europe, and the broader FX. Hence, the more proactive the fiscal reaction is from each impacted country, the more limited the growth and dovish central bank impact would be expected to be, he says. "By extension, we're holding on to the EUR/USD long through the [April 2] announcement," he adds. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Gold Hovers at Record High
data.description:
Gold hovered around $3,050 per ounce on Thursday, a fresh record, supported by rising US rate cut expectations and strong safe-haven demand.

The Federal Reserve noted increased economic uncertainty but still expects to cut rates by 50 bps this year, matching its December projection and boosting non-yielding gold.

Meanwhile, tensions in the Middle East escalated as Israel launched “targeted ground activities” in Gaza, retaking part of a key area after an airstrike ended a two-month ceasefire with Hamas.

The US also kept striking Houthi targets in Yemen after Red Sea attacks, with Trump vowing to hold Iran accountable for future incidents.

At the same time, worries over global trade disputes persisted as new duties are set to take effect in April, following the US’s 25% tariff on steel and aluminum enacted in February.

data.title: Muyuan Foods Returns to Profit in 2024
data.description:
Muyuan Foods returned to an attributable profit of 17.9 billion yuan in 2024 from a loss of 4.26 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

The pork producer returned to earnings per share of 3.24 yuan from 0.79 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 24% to 137.9 billion yuan from 110.9 billion yuan a year earlier.

Shares slipped 1% during Thursday's morning trading.

1705 GMT / 1305 Eastern time:

  • Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem gives a speech at Calgary Economic Development
  • topic is: Tariff uncertainty and monetary policy
  • this will be followed by a Q&A

There will be a live broadcast (the kids call them webcasts now)

data.title: Sinopec Shanghai Petrochemical Swings to 2024 Profit, Revenue Down 6%
data.description:
Sinopec Shanghai Petrochemical swung to net profit attributable to shareholders of 316.5 million yuan, or 0.03 yuan per share, from a loss of 1.41 billion yuan, or 0.13 yuan per share, the previous year.

The petrochemical company's revenue was down 6.3% year over year to 87.1 billion yuan from 93.0 billion yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The company declared a dividend of 0.02 yuan per share for 2024.

data.title: The Trendlines Terminates Subsidiary Employee Involved in Funds Misappropriation
data.description:
The Trendlines terminated the employment of Anton Wibowo, chief executive officer of its subsidiary Trendlines Agrifood Innovation Centre, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The termination follows the conclusion of an independent investigation into the extent of misappropriations between Jan 1, 2023, to Dec. 31, 2024.

Following the conclusion of these investigations, Anton was fired from his role while the group will now consider its legal options.

Meanwhile, Stephen Rhodes, the group's founder, has assumed the role of the subsidiary's CEO on an interim basis.

data.title: Market Chatter: HSBC in Talks to Sell German Fund Administration Business
data.description:
HSBC Holdings is in advanced talks to offload its German fund administration business to Blackfin Capital Partners as it seeks to focus on its core Asian operations, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Blackfin is reportedly working on the final terms of the deal to acquire HSBC's Inka unit, which manages assets worth 400 billion euros. A deal may be reached in the coming weeks, the publication said, citing the people said.

BNP Paribas is also in negotiations for HSBC's German custodian business, Bloomberg said, citing sources. More interested parties may also negotiate for the platforms, which are expected to fetch several hundred million euros, Bloomberg said.

"We are reviewing our strategic options with regard to our securities services business in Germany," HSBC told Bloomberg. "No decision has yet been made, so we will not comment further on this."

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)
